summary:
Clindamycin is recommended as an effective and safe treatment for bacterial vaginosis during pregnancy due to its recent approval as a one-time dose vaginal gel. This aligns well with current guidelines supporting appropriate use based on symptoms rather than universal screening. To summarize:

Clindamycin remains the preferred choice for managing bacterial vaginosis in pregnant women because it offers a convenient single-dose regimen supported by updated regulatory endorsements; this approach balances effectiveness with ease of administration while conforming to guideline recommendations focused on symptomatic patients over broad-screening practices.
